     1	import { daysSinceLastChange, commitsSinceLastChange } from "../../git.js";
     2	import type { DriftIssue } from "../../types.js";
     3	
     4	const WARN_DAYS = 30;
     5	const ERROR_DAYS = 90;
     6	const WARN_COMMITS = 50;
     7	const ERROR_COMMITS = 200;
     8	
     9	/** Check how stale a scaffold file is based on git history */
    10	export async function checkStaleness(
    11	  filePath: string,
    12	  source: string,
    13	  cwd: string
    14	): Promise<DriftIssue[]> {
    15	  const issues: DriftIssue[] = [];
    16	
    17	  const days = await daysSinceLastChange(filePath, cwd);
    18	  const commits = await commitsSinceLastChange(filePath, cwd);
    19	
    20	  if (days !== null && days >= ERROR_DAYS) {
    21	    issues.push({
    22	      code: "STALE_FILE",
    23	      severity: "error",
    24	      file: source,
    25	      line: null,
    26	      message: `File hasn't been updated in ${days} days (threshold: ${ERROR_DAYS}d)`,
    27	    });
    28	  } else if (days !== null && days >= WARN_DAYS) {
    29	    issues.push({
    30	      code: "STALE_FILE",
    31	      severity: "warning",
    32	      file: source,
    33	      line: null,
    34	      message: `File hasn't been updated in ${days} days (threshold: ${WARN_DAYS}d)`,
    35	    });
    36	  }
    37	
    38	  if (commits !== null && commits >= ERROR_COMMITS) {
    39	    issues.push({
    40	      code: "STALE_FILE",
    41	      severity: "error",
    42	      file: source,
    43	      line: null,
    44	      message: `${commits} commits since file was last updated (threshold: ${ERROR_COMMITS})`,
    45	    });
    46	  } else if (commits !== null && commits >= WARN_COMMITS) {
    47	    issues.push({
    48	      code: "STALE_FILE",
    49	      severity: "warning",
    50	      file: source,
    51	      line: null,
    52	      message: `${commits} commits since file was last updated (threshold: ${WARN_COMMITS})`,
    53	    });
    54	  }
    55	
    56	  return issues;
    57	}
=== .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/index.ts ===
     1	import type { MexConfig, ScannerBrief } from "../types.js";
     2	import { scanManifest } from "./manifest.js";
     3	import { scanEntryPoints } from "./entry-points.js";
     4	import { scanFolderTree } from "./folder-tree.js";
     5	import { scanTooling } from "./tooling.js";
     6	import { scanReadme } from "./readme.js";
     7	
     8	/** Run pre-analysis scan and return brief or prompt */
     9	export async function runScan(
    10	  config: MexConfig,
    11	  opts: { jsonOnly?: boolean }
    12	): Promise<ScannerBrief | string> {
    13	  const brief = buildBrief(config.projectRoot);
    14	
    15	  if (opts.jsonOnly) return brief;
    16	
    17	  return buildPrompt(brief);
    18	}
    19	
    20	/** Build the scanner brief from codebase analysis */
    21	function buildBrief(projectRoot: string): ScannerBrief {
    22	  return {
    23	    manifest: scanManifest(projectRoot),
    24	    entryPoints: scanEntryPoints(projectRoot),
    25	    folderTree: scanFolderTree(projectRoot),
    26	    tooling: scanTooling(projectRoot),
    27	    readme: scanReadme(projectRoot),
    28	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    29	  };
    30	}
    31	
    32	/** Build AI prompt with embedded brief */
    33	function buildPrompt(brief: ScannerBrief): string {
    34	  const briefJson = JSON.stringify(brief, null, 2);
    35	
    36	  return `Here is a pre-analyzed brief of the codebase — do NOT explore the filesystem yourself, reason from this brief:
    37	
    38	<brief>
    39	${briefJson}
    40	</brief>
    41	
    42	Using this brief, populate the mex scaffold files. Focus on:
    43	1. context/architecture.md — system components, data flow, integrations
    44	2. context/stack.md — technologies, versions, key libraries
    45	3. context/conventions.md — code patterns, naming, file organization
    46	4. context/decisions.md — architectural choices and their rationale
    47	5. context/setup.md — how to set up and run the project
    48	6. ROUTER.md — update the "Current Project State" section
    49	
    50	For each file, use the information from the brief rather than exploring the filesystem.
    51	Be precise about versions, paths, and dependencies — they come directly from the manifest.`;
    52	}
=== .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts ===
     1	import { readFileSync, readdirSync, existsSync } from "node:fs";
     2	import { resolve, dirname, basename } from "node:path";
     3	import { globSync } from "glob";
     4	import { getGitDiff } from "../git.js";
     5	import type { SyncTarget } from "../types.js";
     6	
     7	/** Build a single combined prompt covering all targets */
     8	export async function buildCombinedBrief(
     9	  targets: SyncTarget[],
    10	  projectRoot: string
    11	): Promise<string> {
    12	  const sections: string[] = [];
    13	
    14	  for (const target of targets) {
    15	    sections.push(await buildFileSection(target, projectRoot));
    16	  }
    17	
    18	  return `The following scaffold files have drift issues that need fixing. Fix all of them in one pass.
    19	
    20	${sections.map((s, i) => `━━━ File ${i + 1}/${sections.length} ━━━\n\n${s}`).join("\n\n")}
    21	
    22	Update each file to fix its issues. Only change what's necessary — do not rewrite sections that are correct.
    23	When a referenced path no longer exists, find the correct current path from the filesystem context above and update the reference.`;
    24	}
    25	
    26	/** Build a targeted prompt for AI to fix a single file */
    27	export async function buildSyncBrief(
    28	  target: SyncTarget,
    29	  projectRoot: string
    30	): Promise<string> {
    31	  const section = await buildFileSection(target, projectRoot);
    32	
    33	  return `The following scaffold file has drift issues that need fixing:
    34	
    35	${section}
    36	
    37	Update the file to fix these issues. Only change what's necessary — do not rewrite sections that are correct.
    38	When a referenced path no longer exists, find the correct current path from the filesystem context above and update the reference.`;
    39	}
    40	
    41	/** Build the content section for a single target (no wrapper instructions) */
    42	async function buildFileSection(
    43	  target: SyncTarget,
    44	  projectRoot: string
    45	): Promise<string> {
    46	  const filePath = resolve(projectRoot, target.file);
    47	  let fileContent: string;
    48	  try {
    49	    fileContent = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
    50	  } catch {
    51	    fileContent = "(file could not be read)";
    52	  }
    53	
    54	  const issueList = target.issues
    55	    .map((i) => `- [${i.severity}] ${i.code}: ${i.message}`)
    56	    .join("\n");
    57	
    58	  // Get git diff for paths referenced by this file's claims
    59	  const claimedPaths = target.issues
    60	    .filter((i) => i.claim?.kind === "path")
    61	    .map((i) => i.claim!.value);
    62	
    63	  const diff = claimedPaths.length
    64	    ? await getGitDiff(claimedPaths, projectRoot)
    65	    : target.gitDiff ?? "";
    66	
    67	  // For MISSING_PATH issues, find what actually exists nearby
    68	  const fileContext = buildFileContext(target, projectRoot);
    69	
    70	  let section = `**File:** ${target.file}
    71	
    72	**Issues found:**
    73	${issueList}
    74	
    75	**Current file content:**
    76	\`\`\`markdown
    77	${fileContent}
    78	\`\`\``;
    79	
    80	  if (fileContext) {
    81	    section += `
    82	
    83	**Filesystem context (what actually exists):**
    84	${fileContext}`;
    85	  }
    86	
    87	  if (diff) {
    88	    section += `
    89	
    90	**Recent git changes in referenced paths:**
    91	\`\`\`diff
    92	${diff}
    93	\`\`\``;
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  return section;
    97	}
    98	
    99	/** For missing path issues, list actual files in the relevant directories */
   100	function buildFileContext(
   101	  target: SyncTarget,
   102	  projectRoot: string
   103	): string | null {
   104	  const missingPaths = target.issues
   105	    .filter((i) => i.code === "MISSING_PATH" && i.claim?.kind === "path")
   106	    .map((i) => i.claim!.value);
   107	
   108	  if (missingPaths.length === 0) return null;
   109	
   110	  const sections: string[] = [];
   111	  const listedDirs = new Set<string>();
   112	
   113	  for (const missing of missingPaths) {
   114	    // Get the directory the missing file was expected in
   115	    const dir = missing.includes("/") ? dirname(missing) : ".";
   116	    const dirKey = dir === "." ? "root" : dir;
   117	
   118	    // List the directory contents (skip if already listed)
   119	    if (!listedDirs.has(dirKey)) {
   120	      listedDirs.add(dirKey);
   121	
   122	      const absDir = resolve(projectRoot, dir);
   123	      if (existsSync(absDir)) {
   124	        try {
   125	          const files = readdirSync(absDir)
   126	            .filter((f) => !f.startsWith("."))
   127	            .sort();
   128	          if (files.length > 0) {
   129	            sections.push(`\`${dir}/\` contains: ${files.join(", ")}`);
   130	          }
   131	        } catch {
   132	          // skip unreadable dirs
   133	        }
   134	      }
   135	    }
   136	
   137	    // Fuzzy search: find files with similar names anywhere in the project
   138	    const name = basename(missing);
   139	    const ext = name.includes(".") ? name.split(".").pop() : null;
   140	    if (ext) {
   141	      const matches = globSync(`**/*.${ext}`, {
   142	        cwd: projectRoot,
   143	        ignore: ["node_modules/**", ".mex/**", "dist/**", ".git/**"],
   144	        maxDepth: 5,
   145	      });
   146	
   147	      if (matches.length > 0 && matches.length <= 20) {
   148	        sections.push(
   149	          `All \`.${ext}\` files in project: ${matches.join(", ")}`
   150	        );
   151	        // Only list once per extension
   152	        break;
   153	      }
   154	    }
   155	  }
   156	
   157	  return sections.length > 0 ? sections.join("\n") : null;
   158	}

=== .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/README.md ===
     1	<p align="center">
     2	  <img width="1024" height="340" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/32ed8985-841d-49c3-81f7-2aabc7c7c564" />
     3	</p>
     4	
     5	<p align="center">
     6	  <strong>Persistent memory for AI coding agents</strong><br>
     7	  <em>Agent-agnostic. Single binary. Zero dependencies.</em>
     8	</p>
     9	
    10	<p align="center">
    11	  <a href="docs/INSTALLATION.md">Installation</a> &bull;
    12	  <a href="docs/AGENT-SETUP.md">Agent Setup</a> &bull;
    13	  <a href="docs/ARCHITECTURE.md">Architecture</a> &bull;
    14	  <a href="docs/PLUGINS.md">Plugins</a> &bull;
    15	  <a href="CONTRIBUTING.md">Contributing</a> &bull;
    16	  <a href="DOCS.md">Full Docs</a>
    17	</p>
    18	
    19	---
    20	
    21	> **engram** `/ˈen.ɡræm/` — *neuroscience*: the physical trace of a memory in the brain.
    22	
    23	Your AI coding agent forgets everything when the session ends. Engram gives it a brain.
    24	
    25	A **Go binary** with SQLite + FTS5 full-text search, exposed via CLI, HTTP API, MCP server, and an interactive TUI. Works with **any agent** that supports MCP — Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Codex, VS Code (Copilot), Antigravity, Cursor, Windsurf, or anything else.
    26	
    27	```
    28	Agent (Claude Code / OpenCode / Gemini CLI / Codex / VS Code / Antigravity / ...)
    29	    ↓ MCP stdio
    30	Engram (single Go binary)
    31	    ↓
    32	SQLite + FTS5 (~/.engram/engram.db)
    33	```
    34	
    35	## Quick Start
    36	
    37	### Install
    38	
    39	```bash
    40	brew install gentleman-programming/tap/engram
    41	```
    42	
    43	Windows, Linux, and other install methods → [docs/INSTALLATION.md](docs/INSTALLATION.md)
    44	
    45	### Setup Your Agent
    46	
    47	| Agent | One-liner |
    48	|-------|-----------|
    49	| Claude Code | `claude plugin marketplace add Gentleman-Programming/engram && claude plugin install engram` |
    50	| OpenCode | `engram setup opencode` |
    51	| Gemini CLI | `engram setup gemini-cli` |
    52	| Codex | `engram setup codex` |
    53	| VS Code | `code --add-mcp '{"name":"engram","command":"engram","args":["mcp"]}'` |
    54	| Cursor / Windsurf / Any MCP | See [docs/AGENT-SETUP.md](docs/AGENT-SETUP.md) |
    55	
    56	Full per-agent config, Memory Protocol, and compaction survival → [docs/AGENT-SETUP.md](docs/AGENT-SETUP.md)
    57	
    58	That's it. No Node.js, no Python, no Docker. **One binary, one SQLite file.**
    59	
    60	## How It Works
    61	
    62	```
    63	1. Agent completes significant work (bugfix, architecture decision, etc.)
    64	2. Agent calls mem_save → title, type, What/Why/Where/Learned
    65	3. Engram persists to SQLite with FTS5 indexing
    66	4. Next session: agent searches memory, gets relevant context
    67	```
    68	
    69	Full details on session lifecycle, topic keys, and memory hygiene → [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
    70	
    71	## MCP Tools
    72	
    73	| Tool | Purpose |
    74	|------|---------|
    75	| `mem_save` | Save observation |
    76	| `mem_update` | Update by ID |
    77	| `mem_delete` | Soft or hard delete |
    78	| `mem_suggest_topic_key` | Stable key for evolving topics |
    79	| `mem_search` | Full-text search |
    80	| `mem_session_summary` | End-of-session save |
    81	| `mem_context` | Recent session context |
    82	| `mem_timeline` | Chronological drill-in |
    83	| `mem_get_observation` | Full content by ID |
    84	| `mem_save_prompt` | Save user prompt |
    85	| `mem_stats` | Memory statistics |
    86	| `mem_session_start` | Register session start |
    87	| `mem_session_end` | Mark session complete |
    88	| `mem_capture_passive` | Extract learnings from text output |
    89	| `mem_merge_projects` | Merge project name variants (admin) |
    90	
    91	Full tool reference → [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#mcp-tools](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#mcp-tools)
    92	
    93	## Terminal UI
    94	
    95	```bash
    96	engram tui
    97	```
    98	
    99	<p align="center">
   100	<img src="assets/tui-dashboard.png" alt="TUI Dashboard" width="400" />
   101	  <img width="400" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0308991a-58bb-4ad8-9aa2-201c059f8b64" />
   102	  <img src="assets/tui-detail.png" alt="TUI Observation Detail" width="400" />
   103	  <img src="assets/tui-search.png" alt="TUI Search Results" width="400" />
   104	</p>
   105	
   106	**Navigation**: `j/k` vim keys, `Enter` to drill in, `/` to search, `Esc` back. Catppuccin Mocha theme.
   107	
   108	## Git Sync
   109	
   110	Share memories across machines. Uses compressed chunks — no merge conflicts, no huge files.
   111	
   112	```bash
   113	engram sync                    # Export new memories as compressed chunk
   114	git add .engram/ && git commit -m "sync engram memories"
   115	engram sync --import           # On another machine: import new chunks
   116	engram sync --status           # Check sync status
   117	```
   118	
   119	Full sync documentation → [DOCS.md](DOCS.md)
   120	
   121	## CLI Reference
   122	
   123	| Command | Description |
   124	|---------|-------------|
   125	| `engram setup [agent]` | Install agent integration |
   126	| `engram serve [port]` | Start HTTP API (default: 7437) |
   127	| `engram mcp` | Start MCP server (stdio). Accepts `--project` or `ENGRAM_PROJECT` |
   128	| `engram tui` | Launch terminal UI |
   129	| `engram search <query>` | Search memories |
   130	| `engram save <title> <msg>` | Save a memory |
   131	| `engram timeline <obs_id>` | Chronological context |
   132	| `engram context [project]` | Recent session context |
   133	| `engram stats` | Memory statistics |
   134	| `engram export [file]` | Export to JSON |
   135	| `engram import <file>` | Import from JSON |
   136	| `engram sync` | Git sync export |
   137	| `engram projects list` | Show all projects with observation/session/prompt counts |
   138	| `engram projects consolidate` | Interactive merge of similar project names (`--all`, `--dry-run`) |
   139	| `engram projects prune` | Remove projects with 0 observations (`--dry-run`) |
   140	| `engram version` | Show version |
   141	
   142	## Documentation
   143	
   144	| Doc | Description |
   145	|-----|-------------|
   146	| [Installation](docs/INSTALLATION.md) | All install methods + platform support |
   147	| [Agent Setup](docs/AGENT-SETUP.md) | Per-agent configuration + Memory Protocol |
   148	| [Architecture](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) | How it works + MCP tools + project structure |
   149	| [Plugins](docs/PLUGINS.md) | OpenCode & Claude Code plugin details |
   150	| [Comparison](docs/COMPARISON.md) | Why Engram vs claude-mem |
   151	| [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md) | Contribution workflow + standards |
   152	| [Full Docs](DOCS.md) | Complete technical reference |
   153	
   154	## License
   155	
   156	MIT
   157	
   158	---
   159	
   160	**Inspired by [claude-mem](https://github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem)** — but agent-agnostic, simpler, and built different.
=== .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/mcp/mcp.go ===
     1	// Package mcp implements the Model Context Protocol server for Engram.
     2	//
     3	// This exposes memory tools via MCP stdio transport so ANY agent
     4	// (OpenCode, Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.) can use Engram's
     5	// persistent memory just by adding it as an MCP server.
     6	//
     7	// Tool profiles allow agents to load only the tools they need:
     8	//
     9	//	engram mcp                    → all 15 tools (default)
    10	//	engram mcp --tools=agent      → 11 tools agents actually use (per skill files)
    11	//	engram mcp --tools=admin      → 4 tools for TUI/CLI (delete, stats, timeline, merge)
    12	//	engram mcp --tools=agent,admin → combine profiles
    13	//	engram mcp --tools=mem_save,mem_search → individual tool names
    14	package mcp
    15	
    16	import (
    17		"context"
    18		"fmt"
    19		"strings"
    20	
    21		projectpkg "github.com/Gentleman-Programming/engram/internal/project"
    22		"github.com/Gentleman-Programming/engram/internal/store"
    23		"github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go/mcp"
    24		"github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go/server"
    25	)
    26	
    27	// MCPConfig holds configuration for the MCP server.
    28	type MCPConfig struct {
    29		DefaultProject string // Auto-detected project name, used when LLM sends empty project
    30	}
    31	
    32	var suggestTopicKey = store.SuggestTopicKey
    33	
    34	var loadMCPStats = func(s *store.Store) (*store.Stats, error) {
    35		return s.Stats()
    36	}
    37	
    38	// ─── Tool Profiles ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    39	//
    40	// "agent" — tools AI agents use during coding sessions:
    41	//   mem_save, mem_search, mem_context, mem_session_summary,
    42	//   mem_session_start, mem_session_end, mem_get_observation,
    43	//   mem_suggest_topic_key, mem_capture_passive, mem_save_prompt
    44	//
    45	// "admin" — tools for manual curation, TUI, and dashboards:
    46	//   mem_update, mem_delete, mem_stats, mem_timeline, mem_merge_projects
    47	//
    48	// "all" (default) — every tool registered.
    49	
    50	// ProfileAgent contains the tool names that AI agents need.
    51	// Sourced from actual skill files and memory protocol instructions
    52	// across all 4 supported agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Codex).
    53	var ProfileAgent = map[string]bool{
    54		"mem_save":              true, // proactive save — referenced 17 times across protocols
    55		"mem_search":            true, // search past memories — referenced 6 times
    56		"mem_context":           true, // recent context from previous sessions — referenced 10 times
    57		"mem_session_summary":   true, // end-of-session summary — referenced 16 times
    58		"mem_session_start":     true, // register session start
    59		"mem_session_end":       true, // mark session completed
    60		"mem_get_observation":   true, // full observation content after search — referenced 4 times
    61		"mem_suggest_topic_key": true, // stable topic key for upserts — referenced 3 times
    62		"mem_capture_passive":   true, // extract learnings from text — referenced in Gemini/Codex protocol
    63		"mem_save_prompt":       true, // save user prompts
    64		"mem_update":            true, // update observation by ID — skills say "use mem_update when you have an exact ID to correct"
    65	}
    66	
    67	// ProfileAdmin contains tools for TUI, dashboards, and manual curation
    68	// that are NOT referenced in any agent skill or memory protocol.
    69	var ProfileAdmin = map[string]bool{
    70		"mem_delete":         true, // only in OpenCode's ENGRAM_TOOLS filter, not in any agent instructions
    71		"mem_stats":          true, // only in OpenCode's ENGRAM_TOOLS filter, not in any agent instructions
    72		"mem_timeline":       true, // only in OpenCode's ENGRAM_TOOLS filter, not in any agent instructions
    73		"mem_merge_projects": true, // destructive curation tool — not for agent use
    74	}
    75	
    76	// Profiles maps profile names to their tool sets.
    77	var Profiles = map[string]map[string]bool{
    78		"agent": ProfileAgent,
    79		"admin": ProfileAdmin,
    80	}
    81	
    82	// ResolveTools takes a comma-separated string of profile names and/or
    83	// individual tool names and returns the set of tool names to register.
    84	// An empty input means "all" — every tool is registered.
    85	func ResolveTools(input string) map[string]bool {
    86		input = strings.TrimSpace(input)
    87		if input == "" || input == "all" {
    88			return nil // nil means register everything
    89		}
    90	
    91		result := make(map[string]bool)
    92		for _, token := range strings.Split(input, ",") {
    93			token = strings.TrimSpace(token)
    94			if token == "" {
    95				continue
    96			}
    97			if token == "all" {
    98				return nil
    99			}
   100			if profile, ok := Profiles[token]; ok {
   101				for tool := range profile {
   102					result[tool] = true
   103				}
   104			} else {
   105				// Treat as individual tool name
   106				result[token] = true
   107			}
   108		}
   109	
   110		if len(result) == 0 {
   111			return nil
   112		}
   113		return result
   114	}
   115	
   116	// NewServer creates an MCP server with ALL tools registered (backwards compatible).
   117	func NewServer(s *store.Store) *server.MCPServer {
   118		return NewServerWithConfig(s, MCPConfig{}, nil)
   119	}
   120	
   121	// serverInstructions tells MCP clients when to use Engram's tools.
   122	// 6 core tools are eager (always in context). The rest are deferred
   123	// and require ToolSearch to load.
   124	const serverInstructions = `Engram provides persistent memory that survives across sessions and compactions.
   125	
   126	CORE TOOLS (always available — use without ToolSearch):
   127	  mem_save — save decisions, bugs, discoveries, conventions PROACTIVELY (do not wait to be asked)
   128	  mem_search — find past work, decisions, or context from previous sessions
   129	  mem_context — get recent session history (call at session start or after compaction)
   130	  mem_session_summary — save end-of-session summary (MANDATORY before saying "done")
   131	  mem_get_observation — get full untruncated content of a search result by ID
   132	  mem_save_prompt — save user prompt for context
   133	
   134	DEFERRED TOOLS (use ToolSearch when needed):
   135	  mem_update, mem_suggest_topic_key, mem_session_start, mem_session_end,
   136	  mem_stats, mem_delete, mem_timeline, mem_capture_passive, mem_merge_projects
   137	
   138	PROACTIVE SAVE RULE: Call mem_save immediately after ANY decision, bug fix, discovery, or convention — not just when asked.`
   139	
   140	// NewServerWithTools creates an MCP server registering only the tools in
   141	// the allowlist. If allowlist is nil, all tools are registered.
   142	func NewServerWithTools(s *store.Store, allowlist map[string]bool) *server.MCPServer {
   143		return NewServerWithConfig(s, MCPConfig{}, allowlist)
   144	}
   145	
   146	// NewServerWithConfig creates an MCP server with full configuration including
   147	// default project detection and optional tool allowlist.
   148	func NewServerWithConfig(s *store.Store, cfg MCPConfig, allowlist map[string]bool) *server.MCPServer {
   149		srv := server.NewMCPServer(
   150			"engram",
   151			"0.1.0",
   152			server.WithToolCapabilities(true),
   153			server.WithInstructions(serverInstructions),
   154		)
   155	
   156		registerTools(srv, s, cfg, allowlist)
   157		return srv
   158	}
   159	
   160	// shouldRegister returns true if the tool should be registered given the
   161	// allowlist. If allowlist is nil, everything is allowed.
   162	func shouldRegister(name string, allowlist map[string]bool) bool {
   163		if allowlist == nil {
   164			return true
   165		}
   166		return allowlist[name]
   167	}
   168	
   169	func registerTools(srv *server.MCPServer, s *store.Store, cfg MCPConfig, allowlist map[string]bool) {
   170		// ─── mem_search (profile: agent, core — always in context) ─────────
   171		if shouldRegister("mem_search", allowlist) {
   172			srv.AddTool(
   173				mcp.NewTool("mem_search",
   174					mcp.WithDescription("Search your persistent memory across all sessions. Use this to find past decisions, bugs fixed, patterns used, files changed, or any context from previous coding sessions."),
   175					mcp.WithTitleAnnotation("Search Memory"),
   176					mcp.WithReadOnlyHintAnnotation(true),
   177					mcp.WithDestructiveHintAnnotation(false),
   178					mcp.WithIdempotentHintAnnotation(true),
   179					mcp.WithOpenWorldHintAnnotation(false),
   180					mcp.WithString("query",
   181						mcp.Required(),
   182						mcp.Description("Search query — natural language or keywords"),
   183					),
   184					mcp.WithString("type",
   185						mcp.Description("Filter by type: tool_use, file_change, command, file_read, search, manual, decision, architecture, bugfix, pattern"),
   186					),
   187					mcp.WithString("project",
   188						mcp.Description("Filter by project name"),
   189					),
   190					mcp.WithString("scope",
   191						mcp.Description("Filter by scope: project (default) or personal"),
   192					),
   193					mcp.WithNumber("limit",
   194						mcp.Description("Max results (default: 10, max: 20)"),
   195					),
   196				),
   197				handleSearch(s, cfg),
   198			)
   199		}
   200	
   201		// ─── mem_save (profile: agent, core — always in context) ───────────
   202		if shouldRegister("mem_save", allowlist) {
   203			srv.AddTool(
   204				mcp.NewTool("mem_save",
   205					mcp.WithTitleAnnotation("Save Memory"),
   206					mcp.WithReadOnlyHintAnnotation(false),
   207					mcp.WithDestructiveHintAnnotation(false),
   208					mcp.WithIdempotentHintAnnotation(false),
   209					mcp.WithOpenWorldHintAnnotation(false),
   210					mcp.WithDescription(`Save an important observation to persistent memory. Call this PROACTIVELY after completing significant work — don't wait to be asked.
   211	
   212	WHEN to save (call this after each of these):
   213	- Architectural decisions or tradeoffs
   214	- Bug fixes (what was wrong, why, how you fixed it)
   215	- New patterns or conventions established
   216	- Configuration changes or environment setup
   217	- Important discoveries or gotchas
   218	- File structure changes
   219	
   220	FORMAT for content — use this structured format:
   221	  **What**: [concise description of what was done]
   222	  **Why**: [the reasoning, user request, or problem that drove it]
   223	  **Where**: [files/paths affected, e.g. src/auth/middleware.ts, internal/store/store.go]
   224	  **Learned**: [any gotchas, edge cases, or decisions made — omit if none]
   225	
   226	TITLE should be short and searchable, like: "JWT auth middleware", "FTS5 query sanitization", "Fixed N+1 in user list"
   227	
   228	Examples:
   229	  title: "Switched from sessions to JWT"
   230	  type: "decision"
   231	  content: "**What**: Replaced express-session with jsonwebtoken for auth\n**Why**: Session storage doesn't scale across multiple instances\n**Where**: src/middleware/auth.ts, src/routes/login.ts\n**Learned**: Must set httpOnly and secure flags on the cookie, refresh tokens need separate rotation logic"
   232	
   233	  title: "Fixed FTS5 syntax error on special chars"
   234	  type: "bugfix"
   235	  content: "**What**: Wrapped each search term in quotes before passing to FTS5 MATCH\n**Why**: Users typing queries like 'fix auth bug' would crash because FTS5 interprets special chars as operators\n**Where**: internal/store/store.go — sanitizeFTS() function\n**Learned**: FTS5 MATCH syntax is NOT the same as LIKE — always sanitize user input"`),
   236					mcp.WithString("title",
   237						mcp.Required(),
   238						mcp.Description("Short, searchable title (e.g. 'JWT auth middleware', 'Fixed N+1 query')"),
   239					),
   240					mcp.WithString("content",
   241						mcp.Required(),
   242						mcp.Description("Structured content using **What**, **Why**, **Where**, **Learned** format"),
   243					),
   244					mcp.WithString("type",
   245						mcp.Description("Category: decision, architecture, bugfix, pattern, config, discovery, learning (default: manual)"),
   246					),
   247					mcp.WithString("session_id",
   248						mcp.Description("Session ID to associate with (default: manual-save-{project})"),
   249					),
   250					mcp.WithString("project",
   251						mcp.Description("Project name"),
   252					),
   253					mcp.WithString("scope",
   254						mcp.Description("Scope for this observation: project (default) or personal"),
   255					),
   256					mcp.WithString("topic_key",
   257						mcp.Description("Optional topic identifier for upserts (e.g. architecture/auth-model). Reuses and updates the latest observation in same project+scope."),
   258					),
   259				),
   260				handleSave(s, cfg),
=== .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/internal/server/server.go ===
     1	// Package server provides the HTTP API for Engram.
     2	//
     3	// This is how external clients (OpenCode plugin, Claude Code hooks,
     4	// any agent) communicate with the memory engine. Simple JSON REST API.
     5	package server
     6	
     7	import (
     8		"encoding/json"
     9		"fmt"
    10		"io"
    11		"log"
    12		"net"
    13		"net/http"
    14		"strconv"
    15		"time"
    16	
    17		"github.com/Gentleman-Programming/engram/internal/store"
    18	)
    19	
    20	var loadServerStats = func(s *store.Store) (*store.Stats, error) {
    21		return s.Stats()
    22	}
    23	
    24	// SyncStatusProvider returns the current sync status. This is implemented
    25	// by autosync.Manager and injected from cmd/engram/main.go.
    26	type SyncStatusProvider interface {
    27		Status() SyncStatus
    28	}
    29	
    30	// SyncStatus mirrors autosync.Status to avoid a direct import cycle.
    31	type SyncStatus struct {
    32		Phase               string     `json:"phase"`
    33		LastError           string     `json:"last_error,omitempty"`
    34		ConsecutiveFailures int        `json:"consecutive_failures"`
    35		BackoffUntil        *time.Time `json:"backoff_until,omitempty"`
    36		LastSyncAt          *time.Time `json:"last_sync_at,omitempty"`
    37	}
    38	
    39	type Server struct {
    40		store      *store.Store
    41		mux        *http.ServeMux
    42		port       int
    43		listen     func(network, address string) (net.Listener, error)
    44		serve      func(net.Listener, http.Handler) error
    45		onWrite    func() // called after successful local writes (for autosync notification)
    46		syncStatus SyncStatusProvider
    47	}
    48	
    49	func New(s *store.Store, port int) *Server {
    50		srv := &Server{store: s, port: port, listen: net.Listen, serve: http.Serve}
    51		srv.mux = http.NewServeMux()
    52		srv.routes()
    53		return srv
    54	}
    55	
    56	// SetOnWrite configures a callback invoked after every successful local write.
    57	// This is used to notify autosync.Manager via NotifyDirty().
    58	func (s *Server) SetOnWrite(fn func()) {
    59		s.onWrite = fn
    60	}
    61	
    62	// SetSyncStatus configures the sync status provider for the /sync/status endpoint.
    63	func (s *Server) SetSyncStatus(provider SyncStatusProvider) {
    64		s.syncStatus = provider
    65	}
    66	
    67	// notifyWrite calls the onWrite callback if configured (best-effort, non-blocking).
    68	func (s *Server) notifyWrite() {
    69		if s.onWrite != nil {
    70			s.onWrite()
    71		}
    72	}
    73	
    74	func (s *Server) Start() error {
    75		addr := fmt.Sprintf("127.0.0.1:%d", s.port)
    76		listenFn := s.listen
    77		if listenFn == nil {
    78			listenFn = net.Listen
    79		}
    80		serveFn := s.serve
    81		if serveFn == nil {
    82			serveFn = http.Serve
    83		}
    84	
    85		ln, err := listenFn("tcp", addr)
    86		if err != nil {
    87			return fmt.Errorf("engram server: listen %s: %w", addr, err)
    88		}
    89		log.Printf("[engram] HTTP server listening on %s", addr)
    90		return serveFn(ln, s.mux)
    91	}
    92	
    93	func (s *Server) Handler() http.Handler {
    94		return s.mux
    95	}
    96	
    97	func (s *Server) routes() {
    98		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /health", s.handleHealth)
    99	
   100		// Sessions
   101		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /sessions", s.handleCreateSession)
   102		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /sessions/{id}/end", s.handleEndSession)
   103		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /sessions/recent", s.handleRecentSessions)
   104	
   105		// Observations
   106		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /observations", s.handleAddObservation)
   107		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /observations/passive", s.handlePassiveCapture)
   108		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /observations/recent", s.handleRecentObservations)
   109		s.mux.HandleFunc("PATCH /observations/{id}", s.handleUpdateObservation)
   110		s.mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /observations/{id}", s.handleDeleteObservation)
   111	
   112		// Search
   113		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /search", s.handleSearch)
   114	
   115		// Timeline
   116		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /timeline", s.handleTimeline)
   117		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /observations/{id}", s.handleGetObservation)
   118	
   119		// Prompts
   120		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /prompts", s.handleAddPrompt)
   121		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /prompts/recent", s.handleRecentPrompts)
   122		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /prompts/search", s.handleSearchPrompts)
   123	
   124		// Context
   125		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /context", s.handleContext)
   126	
   127		// Export / Import
   128		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /export", s.handleExport)
   129		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /import", s.handleImport)
   130	
   131		// Stats
   132		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /stats", s.handleStats)
   133	
   134		// Project migration
   135		s.mux.HandleFunc("POST /projects/migrate", s.handleMigrateProject)
   136	
   137		// Sync status (degraded-state visibility for autosync)
   138		s.mux.HandleFunc("GET /sync/status", s.handleSyncStatus)
   139	}
   140	
   141	// ─── Handlers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   142	
   143	func (s *Server) handleHealth(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   144		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]any{
   145			"status":  "ok",
   146			"service": "engram",
   147			"version": "0.1.0",
   148		})
   149	}
   150	
   151	func (s *Server) handleCreateSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   152		var body struct {
   153			ID        string `json:"id"`
   154			Project   string `json:"project"`
   155			Directory string `json:"directory"`
   156		}
   157		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&body); err != nil {
   158			jsonError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid json: "+err.Error())
   159			return
   160		}
   161		if body.ID == "" || body.Project == "" {
   162			jsonError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "id and project are required")
   163			return
   164		}
   165	
   166		if err := s.store.CreateSession(body.ID, body.Project, body.Directory); err != nil {
   167			jsonError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err.Error())
   168			return
   169		}
   170	
   171		s.notifyWrite()
   172		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusCreated, map[string]string{"id": body.ID, "status": "created"})
   173	}
   174	
   175	func (s *Server) handleEndSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   176		id := r.PathValue("id")
   177	
   178		var body struct {
   179			Summary string `json:"summary"`
   180		}
   181		json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&body)
   182	
   183		if err := s.store.EndSession(id, body.Summary); err != nil {
   184			jsonError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err.Error())
   185			return
   186		}
   187	
   188		s.notifyWrite()
   189		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"id": id, "status": "completed"})
   190	}
   191	
   192	func (s *Server) handleRecentSessions(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   193		project := r.URL.Query().Get("project")
   194		limit := queryInt(r, "limit", 5)
   195	
   196		sessions, err := s.store.RecentSessions(project, limit)
   197		if err != nil {
   198			jsonError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err.Error())
   199			return
   200		}
   201	
   202		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusOK, sessions)
   203	}
   204	
   205	func (s *Server) handleAddObservation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   206		var body store.AddObservationParams
   207		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&body); err != nil {
   208			jsonError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid json: "+err.Error())
   209			return
   210		}
   211		if body.SessionID == "" || body.Title == "" || body.Content == "" {
   212			jsonError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "session_id, title, and content are required")
   213			return
   214		}
   215	
   216		id, err := s.store.AddObservation(body)
   217		if err != nil {
   218			jsonError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err.Error())
   219			return
   220		}
   221	
   222		s.notifyWrite()
   223		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusCreated, map[string]any{"id": id, "status": "saved"})
   224	}
   225	
   226	func (s *Server) handlePassiveCapture(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   227		var body store.PassiveCaptureParams
   228		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&body); err != nil {
   229			jsonError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid json: "+err.Error())
   230			return
   231		}
   232		if body.SessionID == "" {
   233			jsonError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "session_id is required")
   234			return
   235		}
   236	
   237		result, err := s.store.PassiveCapture(body)
   238		if err != nil {
   239			jsonError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err.Error())
   240			return
   241		}
   242	
   243		s.notifyWrite()
   244		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusOK, result)
   245	}
   246	
   247	func (s *Server) handleRecentObservations(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   248		project := r.URL.Query().Get("project")
   249		scope := r.URL.Query().Get("scope")
   250		limit := queryInt(r, "limit", 20)
   251	
   252		obs, err := s.store.RecentObservations(project, scope, limit)
   253		if err != nil {
   254			jsonError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, err.Error())
   255			return
   256		}
   257	
   258		jsonResponse(w, http.StatusOK, obs)
   259	}
   260	
=== .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/claude-code/scripts/post-compaction.sh ===
     1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# Engram — Post-compaction hook for Claude Code
     3	#
     4	# When compaction happens, inject Memory Protocol + context and instruct
     5	# the agent to persist the compacted summary via mem_session_summary.
     6	
     7	ENGRAM_PORT="${ENGRAM_PORT:-7437}"
     8	ENGRAM_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${ENGRAM_PORT}"
     9	
    10	# Load shared helpers
    11	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
    12	source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_helpers.sh"
    13	
    14	# Read hook input from stdin
    15	INPUT=$(cat)
    16	SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // empty')
    17	CWD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.cwd // empty')
    18	PROJECT=$(detect_project "$CWD")
    19	
    20	# Ensure session exists
    21	if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ] && [ -n "$PROJECT" ]; then
    22	  curl -sf "${ENGRAM_URL}/sessions" \
    23	    -X POST \
    24	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    25	    -d "$(jq -n --arg id "$SESSION_ID" --arg project "$PROJECT" --arg dir "$CWD" \
    26	      '{id: $id, project: $project, directory: $dir}')" \
    27	    > /dev/null 2>&1
    28	fi
    29	
    30	# Fetch context from previous sessions
    31	ENCODED_PROJECT=$(printf '%s' "$PROJECT" | jq -sRr @uri)
    32	CONTEXT=$(curl -sf "${ENGRAM_URL}/context?project=${ENCODED_PROJECT}" --max-time 3 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.context // empty')
    33	
    34	# Inject Memory Protocol + compaction instruction + context
    35	cat <<'PROTOCOL'
    36	## Engram Persistent Memory — ACTIVE PROTOCOL
    37	
    38	You have engram memory tools. This protocol is MANDATORY and ALWAYS ACTIVE.
    39	
    40	### CORE TOOLS — always available, no ToolSearch needed
    41	mem_save, mem_search, mem_context, mem_session_summary, mem_get_observation, mem_save_prompt
    42	
    43	Use ToolSearch for other tools: mem_update, mem_suggest_topic_key, mem_session_start, mem_session_end, mem_stats, mem_delete, mem_timeline, mem_capture_passive
    44	
    45	### PROACTIVE SAVE — do NOT wait for user to ask
    46	Call `mem_save` IMMEDIATELY after ANY of these:
    47	- Decision made (architecture, convention, workflow, tool choice)
    48	- Bug fixed (include root cause)
    49	- Convention or workflow documented/updated
    50	- Notion/Jira/GitHub artifact created or updated with significant content
    51	- Non-obvious discovery, gotcha, or edge case found
    52	- Pattern established (naming, structure, approach)
    53	- User preference or constraint learned
    54	- Feature implemented with non-obvious approach
    55	
    56	**Self-check after EVERY task**: "Did I just make a decision, fix a bug, learn something, or establish a convention? If yes → mem_save NOW."
    57	
    58	### SEARCH MEMORY when:
    59	- User asks to recall anything ("remember", "what did we do", "acordate", "qué hicimos")
    60	- Starting work on something that might have been done before
    61	- User mentions a topic you have no context on
    62	
    63	### SESSION CLOSE — before saying "done"/"listo":
    64	Call `mem_session_summary` with: Goal, Discoveries, Accomplished, Next Steps, Relevant Files.
    65	
    66	---
    67	
    68	CRITICAL INSTRUCTION POST-COMPACTION — follow these steps IN ORDER:
    69	PROTOCOL
    70	
    71	printf "\n1. FIRST: Call mem_session_summary with the content of the compacted summary above. Use project: '%s'.\n" "$PROJECT"
    72	printf "   This preserves what was accomplished before compaction.\n\n"
    73	printf "2. THEN: Call mem_context with project: '%s' to recover recent session history and observations.\n" "$PROJECT"
    74	printf "   Read the returned context carefully — it tells you what was being worked on.\n\n"
    75	cat <<'PROTOCOL'
    76	3. If you need more detail on a specific topic, call mem_search with relevant keywords.
    77	
    78	4. Only THEN continue working on what the user asked.
    79	
    80	All 4 steps are MANDATORY. Without them, you lose context and start blind.
    81	PROTOCOL
    82	
    83	# Inject memory context if available
    84	if [ -n "$CONTEXT" ]; then
    85	  printf "\n%s\n" "$CONTEXT"
    86	fi
    87	
    88	exit 0
=== .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/external/plugin/claude-code/scripts/session-start.sh ===
     1	#!/bin/bash
     2	# Engram — SessionStart hook for Claude Code
     3	#
     4	# 1. Ensures the engram server is running
     5	# 2. Creates a session in engram
     6	# 3. Auto-imports git-synced chunks if .engram/manifest.json exists
     7	# 4. Injects Memory Protocol instructions + memory context
     8	
     9	ENGRAM_PORT="${ENGRAM_PORT:-7437}"
    10	ENGRAM_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${ENGRAM_PORT}"
    11	
    12	# Load shared helpers
    13	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
    14	source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_helpers.sh"
    15	
    16	# Read hook input from stdin
    17	INPUT=$(cat)
    18	SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // empty')
    19	CWD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.cwd // empty')
    20	OLD_PROJECT=$(basename "$CWD")
    21	PROJECT=$(detect_project "$CWD")
    22	
    23	# Ensure engram server is running
    24	if ! curl -sf "${ENGRAM_URL}/health" --max-time 1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    25	  engram serve &>/dev/null &
    26	  sleep 0.5
    27	fi
    28	
    29	# Migrate project name if it changed (one-time, idempotent)
    30	if [ "$OLD_PROJECT" != "$PROJECT" ] && [ -n "$OLD_PROJECT" ] && [ -n "$PROJECT" ]; then
    31	  curl -sf "${ENGRAM_URL}/projects/migrate" \
    32	    -X POST \
    33	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    34	    -d "$(jq -n --arg old "$OLD_PROJECT" --arg new "$PROJECT" \
    35	      '{old_project: $old, new_project: $new}')" \
    36	    > /dev/null 2>&1
    37	fi
    38	
    39	# Create session
    40	if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ] && [ -n "$PROJECT" ]; then
    41	  curl -sf "${ENGRAM_URL}/sessions" \
    42	    -X POST \
    43	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    44	    -d "$(jq -n --arg id "$SESSION_ID" --arg project "$PROJECT" --arg dir "$CWD" \
    45	      '{id: $id, project: $project, directory: $dir}')" \
    46	    > /dev/null 2>&1
    47	fi
    48	
    49	# Auto-import git-synced chunks
    50	if [ -f "${CWD}/.engram/manifest.json" ]; then
    51	  engram sync --import 2>/dev/null
    52	fi
    53	
    54	# Fetch memory context
    55	ENCODED_PROJECT=$(printf '%s' "$PROJECT" | jq -sRr @uri)
    56	CONTEXT=$(curl -sf "${ENGRAM_URL}/context?project=${ENCODED_PROJECT}" --max-time 3 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.context // empty')
    57	
    58	# Inject Memory Protocol + context — stdout goes to Claude as additionalContext
    59	cat <<'PROTOCOL'
    60	## Engram Persistent Memory — ACTIVE PROTOCOL
    61	
    62	You have engram memory tools. This protocol is MANDATORY and ALWAYS ACTIVE.
    63	
    64	### CORE TOOLS — always available, no ToolSearch needed
    65	mem_save, mem_search, mem_context, mem_session_summary, mem_get_observation, mem_save_prompt
    66	
    67	Use ToolSearch for other tools: mem_update, mem_suggest_topic_key, mem_session_start, mem_session_end, mem_stats, mem_delete, mem_timeline, mem_capture_passive
    68	
    69	### PROACTIVE SAVE — do NOT wait for user to ask
    70	Call `mem_save` IMMEDIATELY after ANY of these:
    71	- Decision made (architecture, convention, workflow, tool choice)
    72	- Bug fixed (include root cause)
    73	- Convention or workflow documented/updated
    74	- Notion/Jira/GitHub artifact created or updated with significant content
    75	- Non-obvious discovery, gotcha, or edge case found
    76	- Pattern established (naming, structure, approach)
    77	- User preference or constraint learned
    78	- Feature implemented with non-obvious approach
    79	- User confirms your recommendation ("dale", "go with that", "sounds good", "sí, esa")
    80	- User rejects an approach or expresses a preference ("no, better X", "I prefer X", "siempre hacé X")
    81	- Discussion concludes with a clear direction chosen
    82	
    83	**Self-check after EVERY task**: "Did I or the user just make a decision, confirm a recommendation, express a preference, fix a bug, learn something, or establish a convention? If yes → mem_save NOW."
    84	
    85	### SEARCH MEMORY when:
    86	- User asks to recall anything ("remember", "what did we do", "acordate", "qué hicimos")
    87	- Starting work on something that might have been done before
    88	- User mentions a topic you have no context on
    89	- User's FIRST message references the project, a feature, or a problem — call `mem_search` with keywords from their message to check for prior work before responding
    90	
    91	### SESSION CLOSE — before saying "done"/"listo":
    92	Call `mem_session_summary` with: Goal, Discoveries, Accomplished, Next Steps, Relevant Files.
    93	PROTOCOL
    94	
    95	# Inject memory context if available
    96	if [ -n "$CONTEXT" ]; then
    97	  printf "\n%s\n" "$CONTEXT"
    98	fi
    99	
   100	exit 0
